title: papaver radicatum subsp. kluanense
intro: Papaver radicatum subsp. kluanense, also known as Kluane poppy, is a subspecies of the Arctic poppy (Papaver radicatum) that is native to the Canadian Yukon and Alaska. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and treating skin conditions. The health benefits of Papaver radicatum subsp. kluanense include its ability to reduce pain and swelling, and its potential to treat conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by extracting the resin to create a topical ointment.
